20 February 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Trump Considering Initial Limited Strike on Iran - Reports Sputnik 20 Feb 2026 -- US President Donald Trump is considering a possibility to carry out an initial limited strike on Iran to force it make a nuclear deal, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing sources.
  • US Intends to Complete Middle East Troop Deployment by Mid-March - Reports Sputnik 20 Feb 2026 -- The United States plans to wrap up its troop deployment in the Middle East by mid-March in case of a possible operation against Iran, The Washington Post newspaper reported, citing US officials.
  • Meeks, Smith, Himes Oppose U.S. Military Force Against Iran, Urge Continued Diplomacy House Armed Services Committee - Democrats 20 Feb 2026 -- U.S. Representatives Gregory W. Meeks, Ranking Member of the House Foreign Affairs Committee; Adam Smith, Ranking Member of the House Armed Services Committee; and Jim Himes, Ranking Member of the House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence, released the following statement opposing U.S. use of military force against Iran and urging President Trump to continue engaging with Tehran diplomatically:
  • NYT questions Trump's justification for threatening war on Iran Press TV 20 Feb 2026 -- A new analysis has highlighted the Trump administration's failure to provide a public justification for a new act of aggression against Iran, as Americans remain broadly opposed to war with the country.
  • US lawmakers move towards vote on limiting Trump's Iran strike authority Press TV 20 Feb 2026 -- Members of the US Congress could vote as soon as next week on legislation aimed at blocking President Donald Trump from launching military strikes against Iran without explicit congressional approval.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • War Department Announces Realignment of Anomalous Health Incidents Cross-Functional Team to the Office of Research and Engineering DoD 20 Feb 2026 -- The Department of War recently announced the realignment of the Anomalous Health Incidents (AHI) Cross‑Functional Team (CFT) to the Office of the Under Secretary of War for Research and Engineering, effective February 6.
  • Department of War Announces Groundbreaking of New Munitions Campus in Indiana DoD 20 Feb 2026 -- The Department of War (DoW) announces the February 19 groundbreaking on the Munitions Campus in Bloomfield, Indiana. This public-private partnership is designed to expand domestic munitions and energetics production and strengthen the resilience of the U.S. defense industrial base.
  • DOW Enhances U.S. Rare Earth Elements Supply Chain Resilience DoD 20 Feb 2026 -- The Department of War (DOW) announced today a September 3, 2025, two-year, $2 million investment in ReElement Technologies Corporation (RTC) of Fishers, Indiana to address the challenges of sourcing allied raw material and increasing domestic critical mineral separation and purification capabilities, which will help establish a reliable industrial base of rare earth minerals.
  • Department of War Invests $11.8M for the Domestic Processing of Critical Materials DoD 20 Feb 2026 -- The Department of War (DoW) announced today a January 30, 2026, investment of $11.8 million in Defense Production Act (DPA) Title III funds to Umicore Optical Materials USA Inc (UOM).
  • This Week in DOW: Securing the Seas, Arsenal of Freedom, Nuclear Power for the Win DoD News 20 Feb 2026 -- The U.S. military was busy again this week on the world's oceans, stopping narco-terrorists and enforcing an embargo on Venezuelan oil shipments.
  • KFOR Engineers Lay the Groundwork for a Safer Future in Kosovo Army News 20 Feb 2026 -- Engineering within Kosovo Forces Regional Command-East (KFOR RC-E) is not only about clearing obstacles; it is about laying the groundwork for a safe and secure future.
  • Army activates new command for ammunition and energetics production Army News 20 Feb 2026 -- The U.S. Army has established the Capability Program Executive for Ammunition and Energetics (CPE A&E) at Picatinny Arsenal, under the command of Col. Jason Bohannon. The new CPE was formerly the Joint Program Executive Office for Armaments and Ammunition (JPEO A&A). The move is a key part of broader Continuous Transformation efforts intended to increase efficiency and accelerate the delivery of critical capabilities to the warfighter.
  • U.S. Navy, Estonian Partners Conduct Ice Diving Training to Enhance Baltic Maritime Readiness US Navy 20 Feb 2026 -- U.S. Navy Seabee Divers assigned to Underwater Construction Team (UCT) One, Construction Dive Detachment Bravo (CDD/B), are conducting ice diving and underwater demolition training with the Estonian Rescue Board (ERB) and Estonian navy divers during an annual bilateral ice exercise scheduled from Jan. 31 to Feb. 17, 2026.
  • U.S. Submarine Maintenance Period Demonstrates Forward Sustainment in Australia US Navy 20 Feb 2026 -- Last November, on the edge of the Indian Ocean, just beyond mainland Australia and across the Garden Island Causeway to HMAS Stirling, a U.S. Virginia-class submarine quietly completed the first submarine maintenance period without the support of a U.S. submarine tender—a specialized vessel that provides mobile repair and supply services.
  • Fort Gordon to gain ANG cyber operations squadrons US Air Force 20 Feb 2026 -- The Department of the Air Force has selected Fort Gordon, Georgia, as the preferred location for two Air National Guard cyber operations squadrons.
  • Des Moines ANGB to gain ANG Cyber Operations Squadrons US Air Force 20 Feb 2026 -- The Department of the Air Force has selected Des Moines Air National Guard Base, Iowa, as the preferred location to convert re-missioned manpower to support Air National Guard cyber operations.

  • Senior Allied Officials meet in Istanbul to boost NATO's efforts to address Security Challenges in the Southern Neighbourhood NATO 20 Feb 2026 -- The NATO Secretary General's Special Representative for the Southern Neighbourhood, Javier Colomina, convened a meeting of senior Allied officials on the Southern Neighbourhood, on 18 February 2026 in Istanbul, Türkiye. Organised in cooperation with the Turkish Ministry of Foreign Affairs, the meeting featured the participation of Türkiye's Deputy Foreign Minister, Ambassador Z. Levent Gümrükçü.
  • STEADFAST DART 26 demonstrates NATO combined arms capability NATO JFC Brunssum 20 Feb 2026 -- NATO forces displayed how modern multi-domain operations are conducted, as part of a live capability demonstration on Feb. 20, 2026, at the conclusion of exercise STEADFAST DART 26 in northern Germany.
  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna in meeting with Chair of the NATO Military Committee: the most serious threat to NATO can best be deterred by increasing defence spending and capabilities Estonia MFA 20 Feb 2026 -- Today, 20 February, Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna met Admiral Giuseppe Cavo Dragone, Chair of the NATO Military Committee, who is on a visit to Estonia.
  • Chair of the NATO Military Committee visited Estonia Estonia Defence Forces 20 Feb 2026 -- This week, the Chair of the NATO Military Committee (CMC), Admiral Giuseppe Cavo Dragone visited Estonia, where he met with the Commander of the Estonian Defence Forces Lieutenant General Andrus Merilo, and Minister of Defence Hanno Pevkur, and received an overview of the security situation in the region.

  • Four Years Of The Ukraine Invasion: Has Russia's Military Learned To Fight A Better War? RFE/RL 20 Feb 2026 -- Prior to Ukraine, the last time Russia all-out invaded another sovereign country was Georgia. Moscow was victorious after the 16-day conflict in 2008, but it was messy and showed that Russia's armed forces needed a major upgrade.
  • Opening ceremony of MILAN-2026 international exercise takes place at the port of Visakhapatnam in India Russia MoD 20 Feb 2026 -- The opening ceremony of the MYLAN-2026 multinational naval exercise took place at the port of Visakhapatnam (the Republic of India). During the opening ceremony of the exercise, the parade of the crews, involved in the exercise, took place.

  • NATO Deputy Secretary General welcomes progress on defence investment and support to Ukraine with the E5 NATO 20 Feb 2026 -- On Friday (20 February 2026), NATO Deputy Secretary General Radmila Shekerinska joined the European Group of Five (E5) in Krakow, Poland, to discuss efforts to strengthen Euro-Atlantic security and NATO's deterrence and defence, hybrid threats, and ongoing support to Ukraine. Representatives from France, Germany, Italy, Poland, the United Kingdom, and the European Union, attended the E5 meeting.
  • Artificial intelligence and ethical hackers stress-test the DOT-Chain military supply IT system Ukraine MOD 20 Feb 2026
  • Armed Forces of Ukraine simplify and accelerate fuel supply to units Ukraine MOD 20 Feb 2026

  • Major shipbuilding contracts underpin Army's transformation Australia DOD 20 Feb 2026 -- The Albanese Government has taken another significant step towards the Australian Army's transformation to a littoral fleet, with the signing of two key contracts for Landing Craft Heavy and Landing Craft Medium.

  • Canada advances Defence Industrial Strategy to strengthen security, sovereignty and prosperity Canada 20 Feb 2026 -- Canada is pursuing a long-term, whole-of-government approach to strengthening the defence industrial base as a pillar of national security, economic resilience and prosperity.
  • Minister McGuinty concludes visit to British Columbia to advance Canada's new Defence Industrial Strategy Canada 20 Feb 2026 -- Today, the Honourable David J. McGuinty, Minister of National Defence, concluded a productive visit to British Columbia where he met with members of the Canadian defence industry to advance Canada's new Defence Industrial Strategy.

  • INDIA ASSUMES CHAIRMANSHIP OF THE INDIAN OCEAN NAVAL SYMPOSIUM India PIB 20 Feb 2026 -- The 9th Conclave of Chiefs of the Indian Ocean Naval Symposium (IONS) convened at Visakhapatnam on 20 Feb 2026, bringing together Chiefs of Navies and Heads of Maritime Security Agencies from 33 countries, representing IONS Members, Observers, and other Indian Ocean littoral nations
  • INDIAN ARMY HOSTS NATIONAL SEMINAR ON "ENHANCING MILITARY DECISION-MAKING THROUGH WARGAMING AND SIMULATION" AND RELEASES INDIGENOUS DECISION-SUPPORT APPLICATIONS India PIB 20 Feb 2026 -- The 9th Conclave of Chiefs of the Indian Ocean Naval Symposium (IONS) convened at Visakhapatnam on 20 Feb 2026, bringing together Chiefs of Navies and Heads of Maritime Security Agencies from 33 countries, representing IONS Members, Observers, and other Indian Ocean littoral nations.

  • Iranian Destroyer Dena docks at Indian port to participate in MILAN 2026 drills IRNA 20 Feb 2026 -- The destroyer "Dena" has docked at Visakhapatnam Port, eastern India, to participate in the international MILAN 2026 exercise, aimed at developing maritime diplomacy, expanding defense interactions, and showing operational capabilities of the Iranian navy in international waters.
  • Iran's Dena destroyer docks in Indian port as Tehran, New Delhi discuss maritime security Press TV 20 Feb 2026 -- Iran's Dena destroyer has docked in the Indian port of Visakhapatnam to take part in an international naval drill as senior naval commanders from Tehran and New Delhi held talks on strengthening maritime security cooperation.
  • Iran, Russia Conduct Vessel Rescue Exercise in Gulf of Oman Sputnik 20 Feb 2026 -- The Russian and Iranian navies successfully practiced rescuing a vessel hijacked by pirates in the Gulf of Oman during exercises on Thursday, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said.

  • E5 Ministers of Defence meeting: Press conference by High Representative Kaja Kallas European External Action Service (EEAS) 20 Feb 2026
  • Strengthening allied relations and the will to act within the E5 Group Poland - Ministry of National Defence 20 Feb 2026 -- On 20 February, the minister of national defence hosted in Cracow the ministers of defence of France, Germany, Italy and Great Britain.

  • UK and European allies to develop low-cost air defence weapons to protect NATO skies UK MOD 20 Feb 2026 -- European skies to be defended by advanced surface-to-air weapon under new multinational scheme.

  • DFA Statement Philippines DFA 20 Feb 2026 -- The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) is pleased to report on recent developments in pursuit of the four core dynamics of Philippine maritime statecraft.
  • AFP reiterates defense ties with Thai Armed Forces Philippine News Agency 20 Feb 2026 -- The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) has reiterated its commitment to further enhance its defense ties with the Kingdom of Thailand as ranking officials of the Filipino military joined the celebration of Royal Thai Armed Forces Day Reception in Manila.
  • PH-Canada deal vital in addressing cyber threats, Palace says Philippine News Agency 20 Feb 2026 -- Malacañang on Friday emphasized the importance of the Philippines' cyber defense partnership with Canada in strengthening the country's national cybersecurity posture amid the evolving cyber threats.
  • DND, AFP join UK-hosted maritime cybersecurity training Philippine News Agency 20 Feb 2026 -- As part of ongoing efforts to beef up the country's cyber defenses, personnel of the Department of National Defense (DND) and Armed Forces of the Philippines took part in a two-day United Kingdom-hosted maritime cybersecurity training aimed at boosting the Philippines' cyber defense posture within the maritime domain.

  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Kosovo President Osmani US Dept. of State 20 Feb 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with UK Foreign Secretary Cooper US Dept. of State 20 Feb 2026

Defense Industry

  • Contracts for Feb. 20, 2026
  • Nuclear Weapons Engineer Sentenced for Orchestrating 15-Year Kickback Scheme US Dept. of Justice 20 Feb 2026 -- A Kansas man was sentenced yesterday to 29 months in prison for conspiring to fraudulently steer and award subcontracts by a major engineering firm for work on nuclear weapons manufacturing projects for the National Nuclear Security Administration's Kansas City National Security Campus (KCNSC).

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 20 February 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 20 Feb 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Hamas slams Israeli restrictions on access to al-Aqsa mosque during Ramadan Press TV 20 Feb 2026 -- The Palestinian resistance movement Hamas says Israeli restrictions on access to the al-Aqsa Mosque compound on the first Friday of Ramadan have failed to break the will of the Palestinian nation.

  • Israel steps up bloodshed in Lebanon Press TV 20 Feb 2026 -- At least nine people have been killed and 25 others injured in a series of Israeli airstrikes targeting eastern Lebanon.
  • Two killed, several injured in Israeli strike on refugee camp in Lebanon Press TV 20 Feb 2026 -- Israel has conducted a drone strike on the Ain al-Hilweh refugee camp in Lebanon's southern city of Saida, leaving two people killed and several others injured.

  • Trump plans to establish 'massive' military base in southern Gaza: Report Press TV 20 Feb 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has plans to set up a large military base in the Gaza Strip to serve as the "military operating base" for thousands of international troops, a report says.

  • Kazakh, Uzbek Leaders Seek Trump Approval With Gaza Peace Pledges RFE/RL 20 Feb 2026 -- The decision by Kazakhstan and Uzbekistan to join US allies in the Arab world in providing cash and other help - possibly including troops - for the reconstruction of Gaza marks another moment in efforts to forge a new, close relationship with US President Donald Trump.
  • ROK Government Representative's Attendance at Inaugural Meeting of Board of Peace (Feb 19) ROK MFA 20 Feb 2026

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, South Sudan, Democratic Republic of the Congo UNOCHA 20 Feb 2026

  • Next SCS code of conduct negotiations set in Singapore this month Philippine News Agency 20 Feb 2026 -- The next round of negotiations on a South China Sea (SCS) code of conduct (COC) will be held in the last week of February, the Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) said Friday.
  • DFA reasserts Philippines' claim over Sabah Philippine News Agency 20 Feb 2026 -- The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) has reaffirmed the Philippines' claim over Sabah amid renewed discussion over a 2012 Philippine map depicting the contested territory.
  • US military conducts activities in Yellow Sea; PLA effectively responds and handles situation: sources Global Times 20 Feb 2026 -- According to sources, the US military recently organized aircraft to conduct activities in the airspace facing China over the Yellow Sea.
  • Shaping up in the South China Sea Australia DOD 20 Feb 2026 -- It was a day of poise, professionalism, and precision as three partner nations sailed and flew in formation off the west coast of the Philippine island of Luzon during the latest multilateral maritime cooperative activity (MCA).

  • Statement by Spokesperson of Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Republic of Korea on Japanese Foreign Minister's Remarks Regarding Dokdo in Foreign Policy Speech ROK MFA 20 Feb 2026

  • The children of Syria deserve a future worthy of their strength UNICEF 20 Feb 2026 -- This is a summary of what was said by UNICEF Goodwill Ambassador Muzoon Almellehan - to whom quoted text may be attributed - at today's press briefing at the Palais des Nations in Geneva

  • 'Alarming increase' in recruitment of children into gangs in Haiti UN News 20 Feb 2026 -- There has been an "alarming increase" in the number of children being recruited into gangs in Haiti with "devastating consequences" for children, families and society as a whole, the UN reported on Friday.
  • Child trafficking by gangs is putting Haiti's future at risk - UN report OHCHR 20 Feb 2026 -- A UN report published on Friday details the brutal and widespread trafficking of children by Haitian gangs, warning their actions threaten not only the wellbeing of today's children but also of future generations.

  • OSCE Chairman-in-Office Cassis concludes visit to Bosnia and Herzegovina OSCE 20 Feb 2026 -- The OSCE Chairman-in-Office, Switzerland's Federal Councillor Ignazio Cassis, today concluded his visit to Sarajevo, where he met with members of the Bosnia and Herzegovina Presidency, the Minister of Foreign Affairs, and leadership of the Central Election Commission. He also visited the OSCE Mission to Bosnia and Herzegovina to discuss the Organization's ongoing work in the country.

  • China calls for lasting, effective solution to Sudan conflict: envoy People's Daily 20 Feb 2026 -- China on Thursday called on the international community to uphold the five-point consensus in its collective effort to seek a lasting and effective solution to the Sudan conflict, which has inflicted immense suffering on the country and its people, a Chinese envoy said.

  • WFP warns of catastrophic shortfalls in Somalia with millions at risk of deepening hunger crisis WFP 20 Feb 2026 -- The United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) is sounding the alarm that its life‑saving emergency food and nutrition assistance in Somalia is at imminent risk of grinding to a halt without new immediate funding commitments. The organization's resources are expected to be depleted within weeks without urgent replenishment.
  • WFP Geneva Palais Briefing on Somalia: WFP warns of catastrophic shortfalls in Somalia with millions at risk of deepening hunger crisis WFP 20 Feb 2026 -- This is a summary of what was said by the United Nations World Food Programme's (WFP) Director of Emergency Preparedness and Response, Ross Smith, to whom quoted text may be attributed - at a press briefing in Geneva today.

News Reports

  • US Supreme Court strikes down swath of Trump global tariffs Press TV 20 Feb 2026 -- The United States Supreme Court has struck down the global tariffs imposed by the administration of President Donald Trump.

  • Russian Woman, Who Allegedly Drunk-Texted FBI Agents, Pleads Guilty To Lying About FSB Ties RFE/RL 20 Feb 2026 -- A Russian woman who was charged with lying to the FBI about her ties to Russia's main intelligence agency - and then drunk-texted an FBI agent while out on bail - has pleaded guilty.

  • 'Whole-of-nation' approach needed to fight foreign info manipulators Philippine News Agency 20 Feb 2026 -- To neutralize threats caused by "Foreign Information Manipulation Interference" or FIMI, a ranking National Security Council (NSC) official on Friday said democratic societies must use a "whole-of-nation strategy."

  • Eleven Gang Members and Associates Indicted on Racketeering, Attempted Murder, Murder Conspiracy and Drug Trafficking Charges US Dept. of Justice 20 Feb 2026 -- A four-count indictment was unsealed in the Middle District of Florida charging 11 defendants - all alleged Sex Money Murder-24K (SMM-24K) gang members and associates - with crimes including racketeering (RICO) conspiracy, attempted and conspiracy to commit murder in aid of racketeering, and drug conspiracy crimes. The gang was active inside and outside of the prison system in the State of Florida.

  • Meeting with permanent members of the Security Council President of Russia 20 Feb 2026 -- The President chaired a briefing session with permanent members of the Security Council.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova's answer to a media question regarding the situation around the Convention on the Prohibition of the Use, Stockpiling, Production and Transfer of Anti-Personnel Mines and their Destruction Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 20 Feb 2026

  • Taiwan welcomes Japanese FM's remarks on Taiwan Strait peace CNA 20 Feb 2026 -- Taiwan's Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MOFA) on Friday welcomed Japanese Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi's remarks emphasizing the importance of peace and stability in the Taiwan Strait.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 20 Feb 2026

  • CPC Central Committee congratulates DPRK on WPK's Ninth Congress Global Times 20 Feb 2026 -- The Communist Party of China (CPC) Central Committee on Thursday sent a message of congratulations on the convening of the Ninth Congress of the Workers' Party of Korea (WPK).
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un's Opening Address at Ninth Congress of WPK KCNA 20 Feb 2026 -- The respected Comrade Kim Jong Un made an opening address at the Ninth Congress of the Workers' Party of Korea.
  • Ninth Congress of WPK Opens with Splendor KCNA 20 Feb 2026 -- All the Party members and other people in the DPRK, absolutely convinced that the Korean revolution would always emerge victorious thanks to the outstanding ideas, strong fighting efficiency and seasoned leadership of the Workers' Party of Korea, extend the highest tribute and warm congratulations to the Ninth Congress of the WPK, a political event to be specially recorded in the ever-victorious history of the Party's guidance.
  • Top DPRK leader attends ceremony of presenting rocket launchers People's Daily 20 Feb 2026 -- Kim Jong Un, top leader of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK), attended a ceremony of presenting large-caliber multiple rocket launchers in the capital ahead of an upcoming major party congress, the official Korean Central News Agency (KCNA) said Thursday.

  • Former President Yoon gets life in prison for insurrection KOREA.net 20 Feb 2026 -- Former President Yoon Suk Yeol has been sentenced to life in prison for masterminding the failed attempt at martial law on Dec. 3, 2024.
  • S. Korea's ex-president Yoon sentenced to life in prison on insurrection People's Daily 20 Feb 2026 -- South Korea's former President Yoon Suk-yeol was sentenced to life in prison on charges of insurrection stemming from his declaration of emergency martial law, live footage showed Thursday.

  • US Expects Iran to Present Detailed Nuclear Deal Proposal in Coming Days - Reports Sputnik 20 Feb 2026 -- Iran is expected to present a detailed and written version of its proposal on the nuclear deal to the United States in the coming days, CBS News reported on Friday, citing sources.
  • Araghchi: Swift agreement desirable for both Tehran and Washington IRNA 20 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Minister Seyyed Abbas Araghchi has rejected the idea of any deadlines for Iran-US negotiations, saying that a quick deal is something both sides are interested in.
  • Tehran says US has not sought zero enrichment, warns war hawks pushing for 'catastrophic war' Press TV 20 Feb 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister has said Washington has not demanded zero uranium enrichment in ongoing indirect talks, while warning that hardliners are attempting to steer US policy toward a "catastrophic war."
  • Iran 'serious' on achieving a 'fair' deal with US, Araghchi tells Lavrov Press TV 20 Feb 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Tehran is serious on reaching a "fair and balanced" deal through negotiations with the United States.
  • Iranian Nuclear Dossier Has No Military Solution, Only Diplomacy — Foreign Minister Sputnik 20 Feb 2026 -- The issue of the Iranian nuclear dossier cannot be resolved by military means, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on Friday amid an increase in the US military presence in the Middle East and an increasing number of reports of possible military actions against Tehran.
  • Iran will not initiate war, but will respond decisively to any aggression: Envoy Iravani IRNA 20 Feb 2026 -- Iran's Ambassador and Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Amir Saeid Iravani, has emphasized that the Islamic Republic of Iran neither seeks tension nor war and will not initiate any war, but if it is subjected to military aggression, Iran will respond decisively and proportionately.
  • Iran won't initiate war, but will 'decisively' defend itself against aggression: UN envoy Press TV 20 Feb 2026 -- The Iranian ambassador and permanent representative to the United Nations has stressed that the Islamic Republic will not initiate any war but will deliver a "decisive" response to an act of aggression against the country.
  • Russia supports Iran's legitimate rights in negotiations IRNA 20 Feb 2026 -- The Russian Ministry of Foreign Affairs has announced that Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov has expressed support for a fair political and diplomatic solution that respects Tehran's legitimate rights.
  • Iran, China, Russia hold trilateral talks on Geneva nuclear negotiations Press TV 20 Feb 2026 -- Diplomats from Iran, China and Russia have met to review the latest developments in indirect nuclear talks held between Iran and the US held earlier this week in Geneva.
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's telephone conversation with Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Islamic Republic of Iran Abbas Araghchi Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 20 Feb 2026
  • Iran: UN experts demand transparency and accountability following nationwide protests OHCHR 20 Feb 2026 -- UN human rights experts* today urged Iranian authorities to fully disclose the fate and whereabouts of individuals detained, forcibly disappeared or missing in the aftermath of the nationwide protests, and to halt all death sentences and executions related to the demonstrations.
  • Trump says Iran has 10 to 15 days to reach nuclear deal People's Daily 20 Feb 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Thursday that Iran has 10 to 15 days to reach a deal over its nuclear program, otherwise "really bad things" will happen.

  • The Daily Press Briefing (02/20/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 20 Feb 2026
  • Meeting between Jean-Noël Barrot and his Cuban counterpart, Bruno Rodriguez Parrilla (February 20, 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 20 Feb 2026

  • Plan to seek presidency won't affect VP Sara's cases - DOJ Philippine News Agency 20 Feb 2026 -- The Department of Justice (DOJ) on Friday said the complaints filed against Vice President Sara Duterte have been resolved and are now under review.

  • World News in Brief: UN humanitarian chief visits South Sudan, shelter fire risks in Gaza, West Bank violence UN News 20 Feb 2026 -- The UN Emergency Relief Coordinator arrived in South Sudan on Friday to visit one of the most under-reported humanitarian crises in the world, as clashes between government and opposition forces continue in Jonglei state.

  • Prime Minister Andrey Gurov: Fair elections are a cause that is greater than any of us Council of Ministers of the Republic of Bulgaria 20 Feb 2026
